description: PGD adversarial negatives centered on the positive sample y_pos (near-miss fix). Change from q217: pgd_center_on_positive=true so the L2 ball of radius delta=1.5 is anchored at data_sample (the actual noisy positive), not at a far noise init. This ensures negatives are within distance 1.5 of the manifold -- confusable for the current model -- rather than starting at dist ~30 and adding 1.5 more. Also adds mse_neg_init/mse_neg_final diagnostic to verify PGD is doing useful work. Otherwise identical to q217.
notes: Key fix over q217: PGD ball centered on y_pos (data_sample) not noise init. Replay buffer DISABLED (use_replay_buffer=false) because replayed samples from previous steps would be centered on old y_pos values -- not the current batch's y_pos. Could re-enable with care later. Key diagnostics: neg_dist should be ~1.5 (not 25-45), mse_neg_init should be close to mse_pos (model initially confuses near-manifold neg), mse_neg_final should be higher than mse_neg_init (PGD increases error). SUCCESS: neg_dist in [1,3], mse_gap in [0.1, 0.8], val MSE < 0.0097 at 100k.
